ai.issueType: CustomButton div lacks button semantics and keyboard support
ai.description: The CustomButton component renders a div with an onClick handler but no role="button", tabindex, or keyboard event handlers. Screen reader users perceive it as a generic container rather than an interactive element, and keyboard-only users cannot focus or activate it.

ai.issueType: Navigation menu missing id for aria-controls reference
ai.description: The ul element that is conditionally rendered needs an id attribute so it can be programmatically associated with the toggle button via aria-controls.

ai.issueType: Tabs tabpanel has broken aria-labelledby reference
ai.description: The tabpanel references an id `wrong-tab-${tab.id}` that does not exist on any element in the document. The tab buttons lack id attributes, causing screen readers to fail when attempting to determine which tab controls which panel.

ai.issueType: Form email input missing autocomplete attribute
ai.description: The email input field collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and violating WCAG 2.2 SC 1.3.5.

ai.issueType: Form name input missing autocomplete attribute
ai.description: The name input field collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="name" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.

ai.issueType: Form phone input missing autocomplete attribute
ai.description: The telephone input field collects personal data but lacks the autocomplete="tel" attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill.

ai.issueType: Form error message not programmatically associated with input
ai.description: Error messages are rendered in spans but not associated with their corresponding inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage. Screen reader users may not hear the error message when focusing the invalid field.

ai.issueType: Form input missing aria-invalid during validation
ai.description: When validation fails, the inputs do not receive aria-invalid="true", so screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.

ai.issueType: Modal Escape key handler empty
ai.description: The Escape key event listener is attached but the handler contains no logic to close the modal. Keyboard users expect to close the dialog with the Escape key, but the handler is empty and does not call onClose().

ai.issueType: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
ai.description: The dialog container lacks aria-modal="true", which prevents screen readers from properly restricting navigation to the modal content while it is open.

ai.issueType: Dropdown toggle button missing aria-expanded
ai.description: The button toggles the listbox visibility but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the current expanded or collapsed state to assistive technologies.

ai.issueType: Dropdown listbox missing id for aria-controls
ai.description: The ul element acting as a listbox needs an id attribute to be referenced by the toggle button's aria-controls attribute.

ai.issueType: Dropdown listbox options not keyboard accessible
ai.description: The li elements with role="option" are not focusable and lack keyboard event handlers. Users cannot navigate or select options using the keyboard alone.

ai.issueType: Loading spinner missing accessible name
ai.description: The loading indicator is a div with no text content, aria-label, or role, making it invisible to screen readers. Users cannot perceive the loading state.

ai.issueType: View Cart toggle button missing aria-expanded and aria-controls
ai.description: The button toggles the cart modal but does not indicate the expanded state or programmatically associate with the controlled dialog via aria-controls.

ai.issueType: Skeleton loading animation missing reduced motion support
ai.description: The skeleton component uses CSS animation without checking for prefers-reduced-motion, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

ai.issueType: Non-semantic interactive element (CustomButton)
ai.description: The <div> element is used as a button with an onClick handler but lacks semantic role, keyboard focusability, and keyboard event support. Screen reader users will not identify it as a button, and keyboard users cannot activate it.

ai.issueType: Loading spinner missing accessible status
ai.description: The spinning <div> conveys loading state visually but has no role="status" or aria-label. Screen reader users will not be aware of the loading activity.

ai.issueType: Decorative badge dot missing aria-hidden
ai.description: The <span> used for the decorative dot conveys no information but is exposed to the accessibility tree. It should be hidden to reduce noise.

ai.issueType: Section landmark missing accessible name (Welcome)
ai.description: The <section> element is used for structuring content but lacks an aria-label. While not a landmark without a label, adding one improves navigation for users browsing by regions.

ai.issueType: Section landmark missing accessible name (Contact Us)
ai.description: The <section> element is used for structuring content but lacks an aria-label. Adding one improves navigation for users browsing by regions.

ai.issueType: Section landmark missing accessible name (Filter Products)
ai.description: The <section> element is used for structuring content but lacks an aria-label. Adding one improves navigation for users browsing by regions.

ai.issueType: Section landmark missing accessible name (Products)
ai.description: The <section> element is used for structuring content but lacks an aria-label. Adding one improves navigation for users browsing by regions.

eslintJsxA11y.description: Avoid non-native interactive elements. If using native HTML is not possible, add an appropriate role and support for tabbing, mouse, keyboard, and touch inputs to an interactive content element.
Visible, non-interactive elements with click handlers must have at least one keyboard listener.
Visible, non-interactive elements with click handlers must have at least one keyboard listener.
Elements with the ARIA role "option" must have the following attributes defined: aria-selected

webhint.description: Elements must meet enhanced color contrast ratio thresholds: Element has insufficient color contrast of 6.65 (foreground color: #0000ff, background color: #e0e0ff, font size: 12.0pt (16px), font weight: normal). Expected contrast ratio of 7:1
